description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a dark wooden rectangular body and rounded edges, evoking a classic mid-20th-century design. The front face features a large vertical speaker grille with evenly spaced slats, flanked by two prominent metallic tuning and volume knobs. Below the grille, there is a horizontal frequency indicator with numbers and a set of small rectangular buttons for additional controls. The sides and back are plain, with the wood grain texture visible, and a small metallic port or connector is present on one side. The overall appearance suggests a sturdy, well-crafted appliance intended for tabletop use in homes, cafes, or retro-themed urban spaces. The radio's design emphasizes both functionality and aesthetic appeal, making it a decorative as well as a practical object.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a large front speaker grille, metallic knobs, and a frequency indicator, featuring a classic mid-century design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the plain wooden surface and a small metallic port protruding from the lower section.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the vertical speaker grille, two metallic knobs, frequency indicator, and control buttons.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a plain wooden surface and a metallic port, mirroring the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a featureless wooden back panel with subtle wood grain texture.
